mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing present values of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate profit forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of instances where unemployment crosses the 4.5 percent threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized private demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in 65% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization tightening that forces firms to raise prices and margins, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 5-day returns in 70% of late-cycle observations or loses predictive power when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes correlate with positive 20-day returns in 60% of historical cases or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3 percent annualized confirms robust aggregate demand that supports earnings revisions and drives positive equity market performance over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDP readings above 3 percent annualized precede negative 60-day returns in 55% of observations or signal loses significance after 2001.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates a soft landing environment that maximizes corporate earnings potential and yields positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to outperform baseline in 60% of cases or interaction coefficient turns negative in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts during a housing market correction signals collapsing construction demand and financial stress that rapidly transmits to equity valuations over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts drops precede positive 5-day returns in 65% of correction periods or signal loses predictive accuracy after 2004.
